data.title: Holley: Transitioning to Comprehensive Solutions Platform Supports Buy Rating and $8 Target
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity analyst Brian McNamara maintained a Buy rating on Holley on April 10 and set a price target of $8.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brian McNamara has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Holley’s evolving business model and new product strategy. He views the Holley Swap Essentials modular engine swap kits as clear evidence that the company is shifting from a parts supplier to a comprehensive solutions platform, taking on more of the integration work that enthusiasts previously handled themselves.

He also highlights that beginning with GM LS and LT engine applications leverages Holley’s strong brand affinity and existing LS Fest community, increasing the probability of successful adoption. By packaging validated components into tiered kits and simplifying a fragmented category, Holley improves customer confidence and supports channel partners, which McNamara believes should enhance growth, pricing power, and ultimately justify his Buy rating and $8 price target.

HLLY’s price has also changed slightly for the past six months – from $2.970 to $3.120, which is a 5.05% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Secured UK Road Funding Pipeline Underpins Hill & Smith’s Medium-Term Earnings Recovery and Buy Rating
data.description:
David Farrell, an analyst from Jefferies, maintained the Buy rating on Hill & Smith Holdings. The associated price target remains the same with p2,760.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Farrell’s rating is based on the view that the latest U.K. Road Investment Strategy, while flat in nominal terms versus the prior period, still secures a substantial multiyear funding pipeline for strategic roads. This long-term visibility underpins demand for Hill & Smith’s U.K. roads-exposed operations and supports management’s efforts to improve the performance of the seven U.K.-focused businesses within the UK & India Engineered Solutions division.

Farrell also notes that, despite recent mid-single-digit organic revenue declines and margins that remain well below the group’s target, the confirmed programme reduces uncertainty and should help stabilise and ultimately enhance profitability. With a clearer workload outlook and the potential for operational improvements as the company executes on this secured pipeline, he believes the current valuation does not fully reflect the medium-term earnings recovery, justifying a Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
